name: Balboa Park Station / Green Yard
summary_en: Balboa Park Station and its adjacent Curtis E. Green Light Rail Center rail yard appear across transit-planning and board records. The 2002 Balboa Park Station Area Plan aims to remake the underutilized station into a transit hub; a 2009 SFMTA project follows up on pedestrian and bicycle access. A 1994 Budget Committee memo places the Green Light Rail Center at 425 Geneva Street as the primary storage for light rail vehicles, holding a maximum of 142. MTA board records authorize Contract No. 1249 for track replacement, not to exceed $31,197,197, and accept a $702,921.38 emergency switch-replacement contract. The station and yard are operated by the Municipal Transportation Agency, whose FY2025 budget line totals about $1.5B.
